summary.what: Prime semantics: pending.count is in-session blocking only
summary.why: Phase 7 is the single most important agent-UX change in v0.23.0. The agent-UX review identified pending.count as the field every agent prompt anchors on — if it remained the raw total under the new gate semantics, agents would silently overcount and waste tokens trying to document foreign work. The semantic flip is invisible to in-session-only cases (Count unchanged) but reframes the contract for everyone.
summary.how: Extended primePending struct with OutOfSession and Stale fields. gatherPrimeContext now calls both GetPendingCommits (for the in-session set that drives Count) and ExplainPending (for the bucketing). buildPrimePending takes both inputs — extracted to prime_build.go to keep prime.go under the 350-line limit. Updated internal/protocol/protocol.go SessionProtocol checklist line to make it clear that the in-session count is what targets zero. Foreign-author commits are deliberately NOT included in the Commits slice — only their count surfaces — because the agent-UX review correctly flagged that surfacing subject lines would lead agents to hallucinate documentation.

notes: Considered showing the foreign commit subjects in a separate Commits-style field — rejected per agent-UX review's specific concern: agents reading subject lines will fabricate what/why/how rather than respect 'not yours to document.' Counts only is the safer surface. ExplainPending errors are deliberately non-fatal because they're a visibility-only feature; if the classify-everything pass fails for any reason, we still ship the correct in-session Count from the existing filtered path. The compact human-output diagnostic ('n out-of-session commits skipped') was deferred to phase 8 (where it sits more naturally alongside the stale-self note) — the JSON schema change is the load-bearing part.
